prompt.value:
You are evaluating metadata suggestions (name and/or title and/or description) for a content item.

The item context:
- URL: ""
- Existing name: ""
- Existing title: "Explain Code in Simple Terms"
- Existing description: ""
- Content snippet: ""

Fields requested: ["name"]

The suggested metadata:
- Name: "explain-code-simply"
- Title: ""
- Description: ""

The recommender was instructed to:
- Generate a name (prompt slug) in lowercase-with-hyphens format, max 50 characters, that reflects the content of the prompt
- Generate a title that is short and descriptive, under 100 characters
- Generate a description that is 1-2 sentences summarizing the content
- Only generate the requested fields

Evaluate the generated fields:
- A name is good if it is a concise slug that clearly relates to the title/description/content, uses only lowercase letters/numbers separated by single hyphens, and is reasonably short (under 50 chars).
- A title is good if it accurately summarizes the main topic, is concise (under 100 chars), and is not misleading or too vague.
- A description is good if it is a clear 1-2 sentence summary that captures the key information, not vague or generic.
- Only evaluate the fields that were requested. If a field was not requested, ignore it.

Return passed=true if all requested fields are good quality. Include brief reasoning for any field that fails.

Tests metadata (title/description) suggestion quality by calling
suggest_metadata() directly. Verifies that the fields parameter logic
works correctly and that generated content is relevant and well-formatted.
